tree.setup: You walk along the Grand Canal in Suzhou with an elderly boatwoman, Grandma Zhou, who has ferried passengers on this waterway for fifty years. Her boat is wooden, hand-carved by her late husband. The canal water is dark and still in the early evening light.
tree.setup_zh: 你在苏州和一位老船娘周奶奶沿着大运河散步。她在这条水路上摆渡了五十年。她的船是木制的，由她已故的丈夫手工雕刻。傍晚的运河水又暗又静。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 这条运河有两千五百年了。你踩的这块石头，两千五百年来被多少双脚磨过——没有人知道。但水知道。水记得每一样东西。你扔一块石头进去，涟漪散了，你以为什么都没有了。但水的分子结构被改变了——极其微小地改变了。两千五百年的船、两千五百年的脚踩在岸边的泥里流进来的尘土、两千五百年的雨——这条运河的水不是普通的水。它记得一切。我在这条水上漂了五十年，有时候我觉得这条水也记得我。不是以任何可测量的方式——是以一种更安静的方式。
turns.text_pinyin: Zhè tiáo yùnhé yǒu liǎng qiān wǔbǎi nián le. Nǐ cǎi de zhè kuài shítou, liǎng qiān wǔbǎi nián lái bèi duōshǎo shuāng jiǎo mó guò — méiyǒu rén zhīdào. Dàn shuǐ zhīdào. Shuǐ jì de měi yī yàng dōngxi. Nǐ rēng yī kuài shítou jìnqù, liányī sàn le, nǐ yǐwéi shénme dōu méiyǒu le. Dàn shuǐ de fēnzǐ jiégòu bèi gǎibiàn le — jíqí wēixiǎo de gǎibiàn le. Liǎng qiān wǔbǎi nián de chuán, liǎng qiān wǔbǎi nián de jiǎo cǎi zài àn biān de ní lǐ liú jìnlái de chéntǔ, liǎng qiān wǔbǎi nián de yǔ — zhè tiáo yùnhé de shuǐ bú shì pǔtōng de shuǐ. Tā jì de yīqiè. Wǒ zài zhè tiáo shuǐ shàng piāo le wǔshí nián, yǒu shíhou wǒ juéde zhè tiáo shuǐ yě jì de wǒ. Bú shì yǐ rènhé kě cèliáng de fāngshì — shì yǐ yī zhǒng gèng ānjìng de fāngshì.
turns.text_en: This canal is two thousand five hundred years old. The stone you're stepping on — how many pairs of feet have worn it over two thousand five hundred years — no one knows. But the water knows. Water remembers everything. Throw a stone in, ripples fade, you think nothing remains. But the water's molecular structure has been altered — infinitesimally altered.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond to the idea of water remembering everything in a quiet way.

options.text_zh: 以一种更安静的方式记得——也许存在两种记忆：一种记在大脑里，可以描述；另一种记在物质里——石头、水、泥土——不能描述，但能感受到。您说的记忆在水的质感里，在五十年来桨划过同一段水面的熟悉感里。不需要证明——五十年本身就是证据。
options.text_pinyin: Yǐ yī zhǒng gèng ānjìng de fāngshì jì de — yěxǔ shìjiè shàng cúnzài liǎng zhǒng jìyì: yī zhǒng jì zài dànǎo lǐ, kěyǐ bèi tíqǔ hé miáoshù; lìng yī zhǒng jì zài wùzhì lǐ — shítou, shuǐ, mùtou, nítǔ — bù néng bèi miáoshù, dàn néng bèi gǎnshòu dào. Nín shuō de jìyì zài shuǐ de zhìgǎn lǐ, zài wǔshí nián lái jiǎng huá guò tóng yī duàn shuǐmiàn de shúxī gǎn lǐ. Nín hé zhè tiáo yùnhé zhī jiān de jìyì shǔyú dì èr zhǒng. Bù xūyào bèi zhèngmíng — wǔshí nián běnshēn jiù shì zhèngjù.
options.text_en: Remembering in a quieter way — maybe two kinds of memory exist: one stored in the brain, retrievable and describable; another stored in matter — stone, water, wood, earth — indescribable but feelable. Your memory lives in the water's texture, in fifty years of an oar crossing the same surface. The memory between you and this canal is the second kind. It doesn't need proof — fifty years is the evidence.
options.feedback: Two kinds of memory — cerebral versus material, provable versus feelable.

options.text_zh: 运河现在还有人用船运货吗？
options.text_pinyin: Yùnhé xiànzài hái yǒu rén yòng chuán yùn huò ma?
options.text_en: Do people still transport goods by boat on the canal?
options.feedback: Asks about commerce during a meditation on water's memory.

options.text_zh: 这条运河是谁下令修的？
options.text_pinyin: Zhè tiáo yùnhé shì shéi xià lìng xiū de?
options.text_en: Who ordered the canal to be built?
options.feedback: A historical question during a meditation on material memory.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 两种记忆——你分得很清楚。但我要跟你说一件更私人的事。我丈夫三十年前去世了。他做了这条船。我每天划这条船的时候，手握着的是他做的船桨。三十年了，我的手已经把他当年刻的把手磨圆了——他留下的棱角被我的手掌磨成了曲线。这意味着什么？这意味着他手工刻出的形状和我手掌的形状已经互相改变了——他的痕迹变成了我的习惯，我的习惯改变了他的痕迹。这条船桨现在既不是他当年做的样子，也不是我一个人的痕迹——它是我们两个人三十年的合作。他在那边，我在这边——我们通过一根船桨继续在一起。
turns.text_pinyin: Liǎng zhǒng jìyì — nǐ fēn de hěn qīngchǔ. Dàn wǒ yào gēn nǐ shuō yī jiàn gèng sīrén de shì. Wǒ zhàngfu sānshí nián qián qùshì le. Tā zuò le zhè tiáo chuán. Wǒ měi tiān huá zhè tiáo chuán de shíhou, shǒu wò zhe de shì tā zuò de chuánjiǎng. Sānshí nián le, wǒ de shǒu yǐjīng bǎ tā dāngnián kè de bǎshǒu mó yuán le — tā liú xià de léngjiǎo bèi wǒ de shǒuzhǎng mó chéng le qūxiàn. Zhè yìwèi zhe shénme? Zhè yìwèi zhe tā shǒugōng kè chū de xíngzhuàng hé wǒ shǒuzhǎng de xíngzhuàng yǐjīng hùxiāng gǎibiàn le — tā de hénjì biàn chéng le wǒ de xíguàn, wǒ de xíguàn gǎibiàn le tā de hénjì. Zhè tiáo chuánjiǎng xiànzài jì bú shì tā dāngnián zuò de yàngzi, yě bú shì wǒ yī ge rén de hénjì — tā shì wǒmen liǎng ge rén sānshí nián de hézuò. Tā zài nà biān, wǒ zài zhè biān — wǒmen tōngguò yī gēn chuánjiǎng jìxù zài yīqǐ.
turns.text_en: Two kinds of memory — you distinguish clearly. But let me tell you something more personal. My husband passed away thirty years ago. He made this boat. When I row every day, my hands grip the oar he carved. Thirty years on, my hands have worn the edges he carved into curves — his angular cuts reshaped by my palms into smooth lines. What does this mean?

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond to continuing together through the oar across thirty years and death.

options.text_zh: 通过一根船桨继续在一起——不是比喻，是物理事实。他的手留在木头形状里，您的手三十年来与他的形状对话——磨掉棱角，添加曲线。这根船桨是你们共同写出来的一段话，跨越了生死。每天划船时手掌读到的是他的信息——被时间改写过，但依然是他的。最安静的情书。
options.text_pinyin: Tōngguò yī gēn chuánjiǎng jìxù zài yīqǐ — zhè bú shì bǐyù, shì wùlǐ shìshí. Tā de shǒu liú zài le mùtou de xíngzhuàng lǐ, nín de shǒu sānshí nián lái mànman yǔ tā de xíngzhuàng duìhuà — mó diào léngjiǎo, tiānjiā qūxiàn. Xiànzài zhè gēn chuánjiǎng de xíngzhuàng shì nǐmen liǎng ge rén de shǒu gòngtóng xiě chūlái de yī duàn huà — kuàyuè le shēngsǐ de jièxiàn. Nín měi tiān huá chuán shí, shǒuzhǎng dú dào de shì tā liú xià de xìnxī — bèi shíjiān gǎixiě guò, dàn yīrán shì tā de. Zhè gēn chuánjiǎng shì nǐmen zhī jiān zuì ānjìng de qíngshū.
options.text_en: Continuing together through one oar — not metaphor, physical fact. His hands remained in the wood's shape, and yours spent thirty years slowly conversing with his — wearing edges into curves. Now this oar's shape is a sentence written by both your hands — across the boundary of life and death. When you row each day, your palms read his message — rewritten by time, but still his. This oar is the quietest love letter between you.
options.feedback: The oar as love letter — two hands writing across thirty years and the boundary of death.

options.text_zh: 您没有想过换一条新船吗？
options.text_pinyin: Nín méiyǒu xiǎng guò huàn yī tiáo xīn chuán ma?
options.text_en: Have you thought about getting a new boat?
options.feedback: Suggests replacing the vessel of thirty years of connection.
A personal question during a moment of intimate revelation.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 最安静的情书……你说出了我心里三十年没有说出来的东西。但我还要再往深处说一点。你看这条运河——两千五百年的水。我丈夫做这条船的时候，用的木头泡在运河里养了三年。运河的水渗进了木头。所以这条船的木头里有运河的记忆。运河的水里有两千五百年所有船的记忆。我的手掌里有这条船桨三十年的记忆。这条船桨的木头里有我丈夫的手和运河的水的记忆。你看到了吗？一切都连在一起。我的手——船桨——我丈夫的手——木头——运河——两千五百年。没有任何东西是孤立的。每一样东西都在记住别的东西，同时被别的东西记住。
turns.text_pinyin: Zuì ānjìng de qíngshū…… nǐ shuō chū le wǒ xīnlǐ sānshí nián méiyǒu shuō chūlái de dōngxi. Dàn wǒ hái yào zài wǎng shēnchù shuō yīdiǎn. Nǐ kàn zhè tiáo yùnhé — liǎng qiān wǔbǎi nián de shuǐ. Wǒ zhàngfu zuò zhè tiáo chuán de shíhou, yòng de mùtou pào zài yùnhé lǐ yǎng le sān nián. Yùnhé de shuǐ shèn jìn le mùtou. Suǒyǐ zhè tiáo chuán de mùtou lǐ yǒu yùnhé de jìyì. Yùnhé de shuǐ lǐ yǒu liǎng qiān wǔbǎi nián suǒyǒu chuán de jìyì. Wǒ de shǒuzhǎng lǐ yǒu zhè tiáo chuánjiǎng sānshí nián de jìyì. Zhè tiáo chuánjiǎng de mùtou lǐ yǒu wǒ zhàngfu de shǒu hé yùnhé de shuǐ de jìyì. Nǐ kàn dào le ma? Yīqiè dōu lián zài yīqǐ. Wǒ de shǒu — chuánjiǎng — wǒ zhàngfu de shǒu — mùtou — yùnhé — liǎng qiān wǔbǎi nián. Méiyǒu rènhé dōngxi shì gūlì de. Měi yī yàng dōngxi dōu zài jì zhù bié de dōngxi, tóngshí bèi bié de dōngxi jì zhù.
turns.text_en: The quietest love letter… you've spoken what my heart held unsaid for thirty years. But let me go deeper. Look at this canal — two thousand five hundred years of water. When my husband built this boat, he soaked the wood in the canal for three years. Canal water seeped into the wood. So this boat's wood carries the canal's memory.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond to the interconnected web of memory spanning millennia.

options.text_zh: 每一样东西都在记住别的东西，同时被别的东西记住——您的手记住船桨，船桨记住您丈夫的手，木头记住运河的水，运河记住两千五百年的一切。此刻我们也正在被水记住——不是旁观者，是新的节点。也许「活着」的意思就是：我正在被万物记住，同时也在记住万物。
options.text_pinyin: Měi yī yàng dōngxi dōu zài jì zhù bié de dōngxi, tóngshí bèi bié de dōngxi jì zhù — yī zhāng wúxiàn yánshēn de jìyì zhī wǎng. Nín de shǒu jì zhù chuánjiǎng, chuánjiǎng jì zhù nín zhàngfu de shǒu, mùtou jì zhù yùnhé de shuǐ, yùnhé jì zhù liǎng qiān wǔbǎi nián de yīqiè. Cǐkè — nín hé wǒ zhàn zài yùnhé biān de zhè yī kè — yě zhèngzài bèi shuǐ jì zhù. Wǒmen yě jiārù le zhè zhāng wǎng. Bú shì pángguānzhě — shì xīn de jiédiǎn. Yěxǔ zhè cái shì 'huó zhe' de yìsi — bú shì 'wǒ cúnzài', ér shì 'wǒ zhèngzài bèi wànwù jì zhù, tóngshí wǒ yě zài jì zhù wànwù'.
options.text_en: Everything remembering something else while being remembered — an infinitely extending web of memory. Your hands remember the oar, the oar remembers your husband's hands, the wood remembers the canal water, the canal remembers twenty-five hundred years. This moment — you and I standing by the canal — is being remembered by the water too. We've joined the web. Not as observers — as new nodes. Maybe that's what 'being alive' means — not 'I exist,' but 'I am being remembered by all things while remembering all things.'
options.feedback: Being alive as joining the web — remembering and being remembered simultaneously.

options.text_zh: 这条运河现在是世界文化遗产吗？
options.text_pinyin: Zhè tiáo yùnhé xiànzài shì shìjiè wénhuà yíchǎn ma?
options.text_en: Is this canal a UNESCO World Heritage site now?
options.feedback: A heritage designation question at the philosophical climax.

options.text_pinyin: Nín měi tiān hái zài huá chuán ma?
options.text_en: Do you still row every day?
options.feedback: A routine question during a meditation on interconnected memory.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 我们也加入了这张网——你说得对。今天我把一个陌生人带到了运河边上，跟他说了我从来没跟别人说过的话。这些话的振动已经进入了水里。一百年以后的水会记得今天的傍晚——虽然没有人知道它在记什么。这就是活着的全部意义：不是做了多大的事、留了多少名——而是你的每一次呼吸、每一句话、每一个手势都在改变周围的世界，极其微小地改变——微小到无法察觉——但改变了。你今天来到这里，你的脚印留在了岸边的泥里。明天的雨会把它冲掉，但它改变了泥的形状——哪怕是一粒沙的移动。你来过了。水知道。
turns.text_pinyin: Wǒmen yě jiārù le zhè zhāng wǎng — nǐ shuō de duì. Jīntiān wǒ bǎ yī ge mòshēngrén dài dào le yùnhé biān shàng, gēn tā shuō le wǒ cónglái méi gēn biérén shuō guò de huà. Zhèxiē huà de zhèndòng yǐjīng jìnrù le shuǐ lǐ. Yībǎi nián yǐhòu de shuǐ huì jì de jīntiān de bàngwǎn — suīrán méiyǒu rén zhīdào tā zài jì shénme. Zhè jiù shì huó zhe de quánbù yìyì: bú shì zuò le duō dà de shì, liú le duōshǎo míng — ér shì nǐ de měi yī cì hūxī, měi yī jù huà, měi yī ge shǒushì dōu zài gǎibiàn zhōuwéi de shìjiè, jíqí wēixiǎo de gǎibiàn — wēixiǎo dào wúfǎ chájué — dàn gǎibiàn le. Nǐ jīntiān lái dào zhèlǐ, nǐ de jiǎoyìn liú zài le àn biān de ní lǐ. Míngtiān de yǔ huì bǎ tā chōng diào, dàn tā gǎibiàn le ní de xíngzhuàng — nǎpà shì yī lì shā de yídòng. Nǐ lái guò le. Shuǐ zhīdào.
turns.text_en: We've joined the web too — you're right. Today I brought a stranger to the canal and told him things I've never told anyone. The vibrations of those words have already entered the water. Water a hundred years from now will remember this evening — though no one will know what it remembers.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Respond to the final teaching: you were here, the water knows.

options.text_zh: 你来过了，水知道——对「存在」最温柔的定义。不需要被人记住，不需要留下名字。只需要——来过。你的呼吸改变了空气，脚步改变了泥，话改变了水。也许真正的不朽不是名留青史——是你经过的每一滴水都微微地、永远地因为你而不同了。谢谢您，周奶奶。水知道。
options.text_pinyin: Nǐ lái guò le, shuǐ zhīdào — zhè liù ge zì shì duì 'cúnzài' zuì wēnróu de dìngyì. Bù xūyào bèi rén jì zhù, bù xūyào liú xià míngzì. Zhǐ xūyào — lái guò. Nǐ de hūxī gǎibiàn le kōngqì, nǐ de jiǎobù gǎibiàn le ní, nǐ de huà gǎibiàn le shuǐ. Zhèxiē gǎibiàn xiǎo dào wúfǎ jiǎncè — dàn tāmen shì zhēnshí de. Yěxǔ zhè cái shì zhēnzhèng de bùxiǔ — bú shì míng liú qīngshǐ — shì nǐ jīngguò de měi yī dī shuǐ dōu wēiwēi de, yǒngyuǎn de yīnwèi nǐ ér bùtóng le. Xièxie nín, Zhōu nǎinai. Jīntiān wǒ lái dào le yùnhé biān shàng. Shuǐ zhīdào.
options.text_en: You were here, the water knows — six characters, the gentlest definition of 'existence.' No need to be remembered by people, no need to leave a name. Just — having been here. Your breath changed the air, your steps changed the mud, your words changed the water. These changes are too small to detect — but they're real. Maybe true immortality isn't fame — it's that every drop of water you passed is slightly, forever different because of you. Thank you, Grandma Zhou. Today I came to the canal. The water knows.
options.feedback: True immortality as molecular trace — the universe remembers not your name but your passage.

options.text_pinyin: Wǒ kěyǐ zuò nín de chuán ma?
options.text_en: Can I ride in your boat?
options.feedback: A request for a boat ride at the philosophical conclusion.

options.text_pinyin: Nín dǎsuàn huá dào shénme shíhou?
options.text_en: How long do you plan to keep rowing?
options.feedback: A retirement question at the emotional peak.

tree.cultural_note: The Grand Canal (大运河) is the world's longest artificial waterway, stretching 1,776 km from Beijing to Hangzhou. Construction began in the 5th century BCE and continued through multiple dynasties. It became a UNESCO World Heritage Site in 2014. The boatwoman's meditation on water's memory connects to the Chinese philosophical concept of 万物有灵 (wànwù yǒu líng, all things possess spirit/consciousness) and the Daoist understanding that 道 (Dào, the Way) flows through all matter like water. The oar worn smooth by two pairs of hands across death exemplifies 物是人非 (wù shì rén fēi, 'the objects remain, the people are gone') — but inverts it: the object has been transformed by love.
